mechanism_narrative: Persistently high federal funds rates increase the cost of capital for businesses and reduce the present value of future earnings, leading to downward pressure on S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:higher discount rates reduce equity valuations
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns show no statistically significant negative correlation with FEDFUNDS levels over a 5-year rolling window, or average returns are positive when FEDFUNDS is above 4%.

mechanism_narrative: A significant and sustained increase in the unemployment rate signals a deteriorating labor market and broader economic weakness, which typically leads to reduced consumer spending and corporate profits, negatively impacting S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.5 percentage points over a 3-month period
evidence_refs: fact:historical correlation between rising unemployment and recessions
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 40% of instances following a triggered UNRATE increase, or the average return is not significantly negative.

mechanism_narrative: Robust retail sales combined with a stable or declining federal funds rate indicate strong consumer demand supported by accommodative monetary conditions, which boosts corporate revenues and investor confidence, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3-month average growth > 0.5% AND FEDFUNDS 3-month change <= 0
evidence_refs: fact:consumer spending is a major component of GDP
regime_hint: consumer-led expansion with stable monetary policy
failure_condition: When both conditions are met, SPX 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of occurrences, or the average return is not significantly positive.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in industrial production reflects a healthy manufacturing sector and strong supply-side capacity, which translates into higher corporate earnings and positive investor sentiment, supporting S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon during periods of industrial expansion.
evidence_refs: fact:industrial production correlates with corporate profits
regime_hint: industrial expansion with INDPRO annual growth > 2%
failure_condition: During periods where INDPRO annual growth exceeds 2%, SPX 60-day forward returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with INDPRO levels.

mechanism_narrative: A high level of housing starts indicates strong construction activity and consumer confidence in the housing market, which has positive spillover effects on related industries and overall economic demand, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over a short 5-day horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:housing market strength often precedes broader economic growth
failure_condition: When HOUST is above 1300 (3-month average), SPX 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of instances, or the average return is not significantly positive.

mechanism_narrative: High and accelerating consumer price inflation, especially when accompanied by rising federal funds rates, signals an environment of monetary tightening to combat overheating, which typically dampens economic activity and corporate valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL annual growth > 4% AND FEDFUNDS 3-month change > 0.25%
evidence_refs: fact:central banks raise rates to combat inflation
regime_hint: inflationary environment with active monetary tightening
failure_condition: When both inflation and FEDFUNDS rise above their respective thresholds, SPX 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 40% of instances, or the average return is not significantly negative.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent and strong job creation, as measured by non-farm payrolls, indicates a robust labor market and healthy economic expansion, which supports consumer spending and corporate earnings, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: When PAYEMS 3-month average change exceeds 100k, SPX 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of occurrences, or the average return is not significantly positive.

mechanism_narrative: Strong and sustained growth in real GDP reflects a healthy and expanding economy, which generally translates into higher corporate revenues and profits, thereby supporting positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:GDP growth is a primary indicator of economic health
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with GDPC1 growth rates over a 5-year rolling window, or average returns are negative when GDPC1 annual growth is above 2%.
